transcript_facts:
* The patient reports elbow pain on the inside of their elbow.
* The patient reports pain radiating down their arm with pottery creation.
* The patient denies any prior elbow injuries.
* The patient reports a pain level of 7/10.
* The patient reports the pain keeps them up at night.
* The patient has been taking ibuprofen for the pain.
* The patient has tried ice for the pain.
* The patient has a history of hypertension, currently managed with 10 mg lisinopril.
* The patient has diabetes, currently managed with 500 mg metformin twice a day.
* The clinician orders a sling for the patient’s right arm.
* The clinician orders 600 mg ibuprofen six hours apart.
* The clinician orders an A1C test.
* The clinician orders a referral to diabetic counseling.
* The clinician plans to increase metformin to 1000mg twice daily.
* The clinician orders a follow-up appointment in two weeks.
* The x-ray of the patient’s elbow shows no fractures or bony abnormalities.
